{{Quote|By the time we landed on Yavin, whatever shock we'd felt at the destruction of the Death Star had been replaced by anger. For months we'd treated the Rebellion like a disobedient child, only to be repaid for our tolerance with treachery on an unimaginable scale. Frankly, I don't remember much of the fighting. I guess we won.|A 501st trooper's entry in a journal|<ref name="Battlefront II">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref>}}
The '''Yavin blockade''', also known as the '''blockade of Yavin''' or the '''Blockade of Yavin 4''', was a military action conducted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] after the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].

==History==
===The siege===
{{Quote|Afterwards, those of us that survived hooked up with a nearby Imperial fleet making a retaliatory strike on the Rebel's base on Yavin IV. It wasn't the best thought out plan, but we were cut off from our leaders, tired, and operating on instinct.|A 501st trooper's entry in a journal|<ref name="Battlefront II" />}}
[[File:Imp_troops_Yavin.jpg|thumb|left|[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] troops on Yavin.]]
After the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], the Rebel Alliance prepared to evacuate the jungle moon, fearing Imperial reprisals. Fortunately, most of the Rebel fleet, including the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]] under [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and the refugee ships containing the Alliance leadership, had not been present during the battle and were scattered throughout the galaxy.

General Dodonna began organizing the evacuation of the Yavin base almost immediately after the Death Star's destruction. However, before the base staff could be evacuated, an armada of Imperial ships including [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruisers]] appeared in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. Instead of attacking, the Imperial ships formed a blockade that prevented the Rebels from escaping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].

In fact, this blockade had been ordered by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] himself, who wanted to capture leading Rebels. The blockade was so extensive, it surrounded the entire [[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]] Sector. Though it was a large blockade, smaller ships could escape, while the large transports and capital ships were prevented from leaving. The blockade was more political than military, because the Empire didn't want the galaxy to know that an enemy was threatening it. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] managed to escape the blockade multiple times. Once to [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]]<ref name="Star Wars 18">[[Star Wars (1977) 18|''Star Wars'' (1977) 18]]</ref>, to [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]<ref name="Iceworld">"[[Iceworld]]"</ref>, [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]],<ref name="Star Wars 9" /> [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]],<ref name="Star Wars 7" /> [[Akuria II]]<ref name="The Kingdom of Ice">''[[The Kingdom of Ice]]''</ref>, [[Keeper's World]]<ref name="The Keeper's World" /> and other planets. The blockade itself lasted six months, with many other events happening across the galaxy during the siege. Sometime during that time, [[Tyber Zann]] searched the debris of the first Death Star, locating several jettisoned pods that contained secret plans to the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']] and other plans. His forces were able to escape successfully before the Imperials arrived in force to eliminate them.<ref name="Forces of Corruption" />

Meanwhile, the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] was nearing completion at the shipyards of [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]]. This mighty ship could take on an entire armada of smaller vessels and would serve as Vader's flagship. In retaliation for the destruction of the first Death Star, Vader wanted to use the ''Executor'' against the Rebels.

===House of Tagge intrigues===
The original commander for the blockade was [[General/Legends|General]] [[Ulric Tagge/Legends|Ulric Tagge]] and was assisted by his [[House of Tagge/Legends|family's]] [[Tagge Company/Legends|company]], led by [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]]. Several [[Tagge battlecruiser]]s helped take part in the blockade. It was Orman Tagge's goal to gain favor with the Emperor, by creating a rivalry with Darth Vader, and humiliating him. Orman Tagge used his vast resources of TaggeCo to develop weapons to defeat the Alliance at [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]].

The first was a station in the planet of Yavin that carried a turbine to allow fighters through the turbulent atmosphere, called the [[Achtnak Turbine Station]]. [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] used this station as a depot to allow them to maintain a continuous siege of Yavin 4. This station was eventually destroyed by Luke Skywalker, who along with Princess Leia was ambushed by the TIE fighters while returning from another assignment.

The second attempt used a new freezing bomb weapon, the [[Omega Frost/Legends|Omega Frost]]. Han Solo led a Rebel fleet through an unknown gap of the Imperial fleet. Luke Skywalker managed to destroy the firing mechanism, and the Alliance fleet attacked Orman Tagge's fleet and led to his defeat. After this failure, Vader held Orman and Silas Tagge prisoner, and thus had leverage over Ulric Tagge, who believed his brothers dead, assumed the title Baron Tagge and retired from the military.

===Admiral Griff's involvement===
[[File:AttackonYavin.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Imperial forces assault Yavin Base.]]
At the same time, Darth Vader was being pressured by Imperial commanders to strike, having lost patience in the blockade and its failure to contain Luke Skywalker and other Rebels. A group of Admirals secretly revealed the existence of the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] being built at [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] to the Rebels. Skywalker led a team to sabotage the completion of the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], though they were betrayed and most of the task force was killed. Admiral [[Amise Griff]], one of the [[Treason/Legends|treasonous]] admirals, was actually a spy for the Sith Lord. Vader killed the admirals for their betrayal.<ref name="Darth Vader Strikes">"[[Darth Vader Strikes]]"</ref> Vader rewarded Griff by placing him in command of the blockade. Griff launched stunning raids on the [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi base]] on Yavin IV. His TIE fighters were slowly winning the battle of attrition. During another mission, Luke discovered the arctic planet of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. After returning, he suggested to the Alliance that they evacuate to Hoth and set up another base there.<ref name="Iceworld" /> A third mission was the [[Mission to Dantooine]], where [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] defected to the Alliance.<ref name="Rebel Strike III">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''</ref>

Around the same time, Princess Leia had attended a conference of uncommitted systems on [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]], where she met with [[Silver Fyre/Legends|Silver Fyre]], leader of the [[Aquaris Freeholders/Legends|Aquaris Freeholders]] and convinced her to bring their considerable fleet of small ships over to the side of the Rebels. Leia also sent [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] on a quest for a near-fabled [[power gem]], in an attempt to use it against the ''Executor''. Fortunately for the Rebellion, the Princess and Fyre's fleet ran the blockade and defeated the TIE fighters.<ref name="Traitor's Gambit">"[[Traitor's Gambit]]"</ref> The Empire continued to besiege the base, launching several raids to destroy transports and kill leaders.

===Evacuation===
{{Main|Evacuation of Yavin}}
When the Super Star Destroyer [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] was finished, Vader led a final strike on the base with his new flagship. At the advice of Luke Skywalker to relocate to Hoth, the Alliance evacuated the last of the base, and attempted to delay the arrival of the Super Star Destroyer. The first attempt failed, and its commander, [[Vrad Dodonna]], fled the battle, damaging his own ship to appear he barely escaped. Luke Skywalker caught Vrad from escaping, but did not want to hurt General Dodonna's feelings for his son. Vrad and Luke then volunteered on a mission to use a [[power gem]] to weaken the Executor's shields. Vrad, however, left Luke behind to escape on his own and sell the power gem. Attacked by the ''Executor'', Vrad was forced to change course and rammed the ship. As the ''Executor'' redirected all power to its forward shields, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] damaged the ship's rear. Unable to strike the Rebel Base, Darth Vader called upon Admiral Griff at [[Jovan Station]] to use the blockade force. Admiral Griff responded by sending a [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bomber]] fleet to destroy the base. However, General Dodonna detonated the temple—the blast destroyed the bomber fleet. This allowed the last Alliance transport to escape.

In preparations for the evacuation, [[X2]] and [[Shara]] were assigned to locate a transponder containing recognition codes to bypass the blockade located within the wreckage of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. Amidst the debris field, the two Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] fought a number of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] over the transponder. The Rebels emerged victorious and managed to reclaim the device.

===Final actions===
Despite successfully evacuating the base, the Rebels were still faced with escaping from the Imperial blockade. Rebel-aligned Admiral Gial Ackbar and his [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] forces attacked the [[Vallusk Cluster/Legends|Vallusk Cluster]] and two other locations along the blockade as a diversion. This allowed a gap in the blockade for the Alliance to escape. Admiral Griff was not fooled, and sent his three Star Destroyers to a nearby star to hide and wait to ambush the Rebel fleet. Luckily, Luke Skywalker discovered the Star Destroyers and warned them.
[[File:Rebels_leave_Yavin.jpg|thumb|left|200px| Rebels evacuate Yavin.]]
Instead, Princess Leia led the fleet through a stellar flare around the star, with Skywalker guiding them through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Meanwhile, with the repairs complete, Darth Vader and the ''Executor'' awaited on the other side of the star to ambush the fleet. Darth Vader had used Griff to draw the Alliance near him, and exhaust them. But the ambitious Griff, foolishly miscalculated a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] leap to catch the Alliance, and his three Star Destroyers collided into the ''Executor''. Admiral Griff was killed, but fortunately for Vader, the ''Executor's'' shields protected him from the collision. This event allowed the Alliance to escape. The Alliance finally escaped the Imperial blockade and spent a year relocating to Hoth and building [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]].
